title: "Violet Labyrinth Puzzle v11383"

description: |
  At 22:05 the **Violet Labyrinth Stone** vanished from its crystal cradle.
  Four badge-holders (1 2 3 4) were logged in the chamber corridor.
  • Exactly one suspect tells *only* lies; the other three speak *only* truths.  
  • Exactly one suspect is the thief.  
  • The universal liar and the thief are two different people.

  Suspect 1  
    (1)  “Were every word of 2 a lie, 3 could not be the thief.”  
    (2)  “Just so you know: the moment 3-(2) is correct, 4 was nowhere near the cradle.”  
    (3)  “Either 2 loitered past 22:00 **or** 4’s timeline is pure fiction—but never both.”  
    (4)  “Whatever 4 claims in their first line happens to match the security logs.”

  Suspect 2  
    (1)  “Contrary to 1’s story, the cameras still show 1 inside at 22:05.”  
    (2)  “If 3 is fabricating everything, then 4 alone is spotless in every detail.”  
    (3)  “You won’t find me on any footage after the clock struck 22:01.”  
    (4)  “Should 4’s closing remark prove false, 1 must be the liar in this room.”

  Suspect 3  
    (1)  “I never set foot by that cradle once the clock passed 22:00.”  
    (2)  “Everything 2 just said in line (3) is untrue.”  
    (3)  “Whenever 2 is honest, you will *not* catch 4 telling the truth.”  
    (4)  “If 1 really left before 22:05, then 2’s first claim is nonsense.”

  Suspect 4  
    (1)  “Actually, I was inspecting the cradle at 22:05.”  
    (2)  “If 1’s fourth remark aligns with the logs, then every syllable from 2 is deceit.”  
    (3)  “Either 3’s second statement is right **or** I am completely blameless—but the two cannot coincide.”  
    (4)  “No matter what 2 asserts, 3 never lingered close to that cradle.”

question: "Who stole the Violet Labyrinth Stone? Return a single digit 1–4."

answer: >
  explanation: |
    A consistent assignment exists only when  
    • 4 is the sole liar (every one of their four sentences is false), and  
    • 2 is the sole thief (yet still entirely truthful).  

    Any other placement of the liar role either produces more than one false-speaker
    or forces the liar to be the thief, breaking the stated rules.

  final: '2'
